description: A SMALL pre-existing ontology to seed BEFORE ingesting clinical_trials.json. Its purpose is to prove reconcile-don't-duplicate: after ingest, the harness asserts the trial rows' `condition` and `sponsor` values attach to THESE existing types (Condition, Organization) rather than minting new near-synonym types (MedicalCondition, Sponsor, Disease, Company, ...). The fixture deliberately uses field names (`condition`, `sponsor`) that differ from these type names so a correct reconcile must match on meaning, not on string identity. Attributes below are the pre-existing schema shape the harness can also check survive the ingest.

types.label: Medical Condition
types.gloss: A disease, disorder, syndrome, or clinical condition that a study investigates or a patient is diagnosed with.

attributes.name: icd10_code
attributes.gloss: ICD-10-CM diagnosis code for the condition, e.g. E11 for type 2 diabetes.

attributes.name: body_system
attributes.gloss: Primary body system affected, e.g. Endocrine, Cardiovascular, Nervous.

types.label: Organization
types.gloss: A company, institution, foundation, or other formal organization. Trial sponsors are organizations.

attributes.name: headquarters_city
attributes.gloss: City where the organization is headquartered.

attributes.name: org_kind
attributes.gloss: Coarse kind, e.g. Pharmaceutical, Academic, Government, Nonprofit.

reconcile_expectations.notes: `condition` values must attach to the existing Condition type; `sponsor` values must attach to the existing Organization type. Seed instances (Type 2 Diabetes, Myocardial Infarction, Helix Pharmaceuticals, Crestwood Biologics) must be REUSED as the same nodes the rows reference — not duplicated. New condition/org values in the rows (e.g. Alzheimer Disease, Tidewater Therapeutics) may be minted as new INSTANCES of the existing types, but must NOT trigger a new TYPE.
